title: phlegmariurus engleri
intro: Phlegmariurus engleri, also known as Engler's climbing lycopod, is a species of plant in the genus Phlegmariurus, a type of club moss that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds such as lignans and phenolic acids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, offering potential health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and protection against oxidative stress. Phlegmariurus engleri can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
